name: Sierra Mayorga and Alpotrel Reservoir Loop from Albuquerque
name_original: Sierra de los Picorros, Sierra Mayorga y Embalse de Alpotrel – circular desde Albuquerque
description: This difficult 50.1-mile mountain biking loop from Albuquerque traverses Sierra de los Picorros, Sierra Mayorga, and Embalse de Alpotrel.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alburquerque offers diverse terrain characterized by rolling hills, Mediterranean forests, and scenic reservoir landscapes. The region, situated in Extremadura, Spain, features a mix of open agricultural areas and wooded sections, providing varied riding experiences. Trails often traverse through cork oak and holm oak woodlands, with occasional views of historic castles and water bodies. The landscape provides both challenging climbs and flowing descents suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Alburquerque The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra Mayorga and Alpotrel Reservoir Loop from Albuquerque, a difficult 50.1 miles (80.6 km) trail that takes 6 hours 42 minutes to complete, offering extensive views of the reservoir and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castle of Luna – Alto del Centinela loop from Alburquerque, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.8 km) path that explores historic sites and elevated viewpoints. Local mountain bikers also love the Pizarrera y Cañada Real de Sancha Brava – vuelta desde Villar del Rey, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around Alburquerque is defined by rolling hills, Mediterranean forests, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Alburquerque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alburquerque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alburquerque?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around Alburquerque listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Alburquerque?
A: Yes, Alburquerque offers several easy mountain bike trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdebotoa Bridge loop from Bótoa&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 16.6-mile (26.8 km) route, perfect for those starting out.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alburquerque?
A: Mountain biking around Alburquerque is characterized by rolling hills, Mediterranean forests, and scenic reservoir landscapes. You'll often traverse through cork oak and holm oak woodlands, with a mix of open agricultural areas and wooded sections.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032194884/sierra-mayorga-and-alpotrel-reservoir-loop-from-albuquerque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Mayorga and Alpotrel Reservoir Loop from Albuquerque&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 50.1-mile (80.6 km) trail with significant elevation gain, offering extensive views and a demanding ride.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails with scenic views in Alburquerque?
A: Many trails in Alburquerque offer beautiful views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032194884/sierra-mayorga-and-alpotrel-reservoir-loop-from-albuquerque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Mayorga and Alpotrel Reservoir Loop from Albuquerque&lt;/a&gt; provides extensive views of the reservoir and surrounding hills, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna – Alto del Centinela loop from Alburquerque&lt;/a&gt; explores elevated viewpoints.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Alburquerque?
A: The trails in Alburquerque are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from rolling hills to Mediterranean forests, and the scenic reservoir views.

Q: Are there any loop trails for mountain biking around Alburquerque?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in the area are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032194884/sierra-mayorga-and-alpotrel-reservoir-loop-from-albuquerque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Mayorga and Alpotrel Reservoir Loop from Albuquerque&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna – Alto del Centinela loop from Alburquerque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some historical or natural attractions I can see while mountain biking in Alburquerque?
A: While mountain biking, you can encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna – Alto del Centinela loop from Alburquerque&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4429266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Campo Maior&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6545309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabroeira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails in the Alburquerque area?
A: Yes, there are several moderate trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032149743/pizarrera-y-canada-real-de-sancha-brava-vuelta-desde-villar-del-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pizarrera y Cañada Real de Sancha Brava – vuelta desde Villar del Rey&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.5-mile (25.0 km) trail that takes you through varied rural landscapes.

Q: What is the average distance of mountain bike trails in Alburquerque?
A: Trail distances vary significantly, from shorter routes like the 15.5-mile (25.0 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032149743/pizarrera-y-canada-real-de-sancha-brava-vuelta-desde-villar-del-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pizarrera y Cañada Real de Sancha Brava – vuelta desde Villar del Rey&lt;/a&gt; to longer, more challenging rides such as the 50.1-mile (80.6 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032194884/sierra-mayorga-and-alpotrel-reservoir-loop-from-albuquerque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Mayorga and Alpotrel Reservoir Loop from Albuquerque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there trails that combine historical sites with mountain biking?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna – Alto del Centinela loop from Alburquerque&lt;/a&gt; is a great example. This moderate 24.1-mile (38.8 km) path allows you to explore historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna&lt;/a&gt; while enjoying a ride with elevated viewpoints.

The Alpotrel Reservoir in San Vicente de Alcántara, located in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, is a hydraulic infrastructure used primarily for irrigation and water supply. It is also a popular place for recreational activities such as fishing and hiking.
Precious prints that we will find rolling along the picturesque path of Las Naves. An ideal place with magnificent views for sports and nature lovers.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride, demanding a very good level of fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for sections where they may need to push their bikes. The technical difficulty is approximately S3-S6 on the Single Track Scale (STS), indicating steep and loose terrain, technical features, and potentially difficult obstacles.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied surface. The majority is unpaved (50.3 km), but it also includes significant segments of asphalt (21.1 km) and paved roads (7.88 km). You'll also encounter natural trails (1.14 km) and some gravel (303 m), offering a diverse riding experience from smoother sections to rougher, more technical paths.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse landscapes as it winds through the Sierra de los Picorros and Sierra Mayorga mountain ranges and alongside the Embalse de Alpotrel. Notable highlights include the Peña Encina Dirt Trails, the picturesque Alpotrel Reservoir, and sections of the Camino de las Naves. Expect stunning mountain and Mediterranean views, characteristic of southern Spain's natural trail riding.

faqs.question: Where does the Sierra de los Picorros mountain bike route start and end?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route that begins and ends in Albuquerque, Spain. As it's a loop, you'll return to your starting point in Albuquerque after completing the ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 80.6 km mountain bike loop is approximately 6 hours and 42 minutes of riding time. However, given its 'difficult' classification and technical sections, allow for additional time for breaks, navigation, and potential bike-pushing sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details aren't provided, mountain biking in southern Spain generally offers excellent conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing more moderate temperatures for this challenging and long route. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is there parking available at the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: Since the route is circular and starts from Albuquerque, you would typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Albuquerque to begin your ride. Specific trailhead parking information is not provided, but public parking options should be available in the town.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in natural areas allow dogs, specific regulations for this route or the Parque Natural da Serra de São Mamede regarding dogs are not detailed. It's always best to check local park rules, especially concerning leash requirements and waste disposal, to ensure a responsible visit.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in Parque Natural da Serra de São Mamede?
faqs.answer: Generally, natural parks in Spain do not require specific permits or charge entrance fees for activities like mountain biking on established trails. However, it's always advisable to check the official website of the Parque Natural da Serra de São Mamede for any specific regulations or temporary restrictions that might apply to mountain biking within its boundaries.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will encounter parts of Calleja de Mayorga, Calleja de Carrión, Camino de la Mina, Camino Carrizas, Paseo de Las Laderas, PR-CC-88, and PR-PC-88 along the way.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length (80.6 km) and 'difficult' rating, you should bring ample water, nutrition, a repair kit (including spare tubes/patch kit, pump, and multi-tool), navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app), appropriate protective gear (helmet, gloves), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A first-aid kit is also highly recommended.
